primary_sources.title: MACC public statement, 6 May 2026: charging recommendation, ARM Holdings RM1.1B chip-design IP licence
Rafizi Ramli press conference and submitted evidence, 14 May 2026

allegation_summary: MACC publicly stated on 6 May 2026 it would recommend charges against two individuals over the RM1.1 billion ARM Holdings chip-design IP licence signed by MIDA on 5 March 2025. The two have not been named. Offences cited: abuse of power (MACC Act s.23), cheating (Penal Code s.420), criminal breach of trust (s.409). Eight days later, former Economy Minister Rafizi Ramli submitted 28 pages of evidence and a USB drive to MACC. The Economy Ministry under Akmal Nasrullah continues the deal.
notes: The case sits inside the larger pattern that the T4A issue 1990 surfaces: MACC investigates, the same government defends the deal, the rule a billion-ringgit IP licence should have cleared is still not public. Azam Baki's tenure as MACC chief ended 12 May 2026 — six days after the charging recommendation. The Attorney-General's Chambers decides whether to charge under Article 145(3).
